title: Phebalium beckleri
taxonomy: Phebalium beckleri is classified in the family Rutaceae, which is a large and diverse family of flowering plants commonly known as the citrus or rue family. It is native to Australia. Phebalium beckleri is also classified as a member of the genus Phebalium, which is a group of small shrubs and trees. The classification of Phebalium beckleri is further narrowed down to the subfamily Rutoideae. This classification is based on the plant's morphology and genetic characteristics.

intro: Phebalium beckleri, also known as the Golden Phebalium, is a small to medium-sized shrub native to eastern Australia, belonging to the family Rutaceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, as well as its ability to act as a natural antiseptic and wound healer. The bioactive constituents of Phebalium beckleri include essential oils, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant are teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions, including colds, coughs, and skin irritations.
